mnemonics.title: TEMPLATE Syntax
mnemonics.content: TEMPLATE = T-ype E-very M-ajor P-rogram L-anguage A-llows M-ost P-rogramming E-fficiency. Remember: 'template<typename T>' or 'template<class T>'.

mnemonics.title: Template Types Remember
mnemonics.content: TEMPLATE has 2 Types: T-ype P-arameters (typename/class) and N-on-T-ype P-arameters (int, pointers). Example: template<int size>.

mnemonics.title: SFINAE记忆
mnemonics.content: SFINAE = Substitution Failure Is Not An Error - When template substitution fails, Compiler Checks Another Option instead of Erroring.

acronyms.usage: Provides ready-to-use template containers like vector, list, stack, queue, and algorithms.

acronyms.expansion: Substitution Failure Is Not An Error
acronyms.usage: C++ template mechanism where invalid template arguments cause overload to be skipped rather than causing compilation error.
Related to type checking but templates work at compile-time.
A C++ idiom where a class derives from a template class parameterized with itself.

visualTips.visualization: Think of a template as a cookie cutter (blueprint) that can make cookies (instances) of different flavors (types). The cookie cutter itself is the template, and each cookie is an instantiated class/function.

Imagine a printing press (compiler) that takes a template (form) and produces copies (instantiations) for each type used. The press doesn't run every time - it happens once during setup (compilation).
Think of a general recipe book (generic template) and a specialized French recipe (specialized version) for when you need a specific approach for certain cases.

analogies.analogy: Like a mathematical formula y = mx that works for any number - the formula is the template, the number you plug in is the type parameter.
Like a building blueprint that can be used to build houses, offices, or hospitals - same structure but different materials and purposes.
Like specifying the size of a container when ordering - you specify '10 slots' as a fixed value, not a type.
Like having a general rule for all vehicles, but a special rule just for electric cars - still a template, but more specific for certain types.
